data.title: SEC casts doubt on approval of 5x leveraged ETFs like Volatility Shares, raising concerns about leverage limits in US market. MSTR stock positive amid shutdown. Flood of 3x and 5x leveraged equities seeking ETF registration. SEC unsure if compliant with 2x leverage cap. Volatility Shares submits for 27 extremely leveraged ETFs.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is unsure about approving recent filings for highly leveraged ETFs, like Volatility Shares' proposed 5x products, sparking concerns over leverage limits in the U.S. market. Following the government shutdown, there has been a surge in 3x and 5x leveraged equities seeking ETF registration, with the SEC's Brian Daly mentioning uncertainty about these ETFs complying with Derivatives Rule (Rule 18f-4), which limits leverage to 2x. Volatility Shares filed on Wednesday for 27 extremely leveraged ETFs, marking the first-ever proposal of such magnitude. Additionally, MSTR stock is displaying positive movement, indicating potential momentum. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SEA token to launch on OpenSea in Q1 2026, with 50% for users, as trading volume rises. #NFT #Crypto #OpenSea
data.description: In the first quarter of 2026, OpenSea, an NFT marketplace and platform for token trading, plans to introduce its SEA token, with 50% of the total tokens specifically designated for the platform’s user community.

data.title: Rogers offering Blue Jays fans chance to win 500 free tickets per game at Rogers Centre for ALCS games.
data.description: In Toronto on October 17, 2025, Rogers, the owner of the Toronto Blue Jays, announced a giveaway for fans. They are offering the opportunity to win 500 complimentary tickets for a dedicated section at every Blue Jays home game held at Rogers Centre during the American League Championship Series (ALCS). This initiative aims to enhance the fan experience and boost support for the team during the crucial championship series.

data.title: KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) beats Q3 earnings estimates, posting 41 cents/share vs expected 38 cents/share, with sales at $1.895 billion vs $1.881 billion. CEO Chris Gorman cites strong momentum, 17% revenue growth y-o-y, and 1,000 basis points of operating leverage.
data.description: KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) reported impressive third-quarter earnings, surpassing market projections. The company's adjusted earnings of 41 cents per share exceeded expectations of 38 cents per share. Quarterly sales reached $1.895 billion, surpassing estimates of $1.881 billion. KeyCorp's CEO, Chris Gorman, expressed satisfaction with the results, highlighting a 17% increase in adjusted revenue compared to the previous year. The company also achieved over 1,000 basis points of operating leverage in the quarter. This growth was primarily attributed to net interest income tailwinds and an increase in adjusted noninterest income. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Judge John D. Bates received the 41st Annual Devitt Award, highest judicial honor, at the Supreme Court.
data.description: Last night, the 41st Annual Devitt Award by the Dwight D. Opperman Foundation took place at the Supreme Court of the United States in Phoenix. Chief Justice John G. Roberts, Jr. presided over the ceremony which celebrated the Senior United States District Judge of the U.S. District.

data.title: BNY Mellon beats Q3 estimates with $1.91 adjusted EPS, $5.081B total revenue. CEO pleased with 9% YoY revenue rise.
data.description: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation, listed under NYSE as BK, announced its third-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations. In 2025, the company reported a diluted EPS of $1.88 and an adjusted diluted EPS of $1.91, outperforming the estimated $1.77. Total revenue increased by 9% year over year to $5.081 billion, exceeding the estimated $4.974 billion, driven by a 7% rise in fee revenue and an 18% growth in net interest income. The GAAP pre-tax operating margin stood at 36%, the return on equity at 13.7%, and the return on tangible common equity at 25.6%. CEO Robin Vince expressed satisfaction with the results, noting that BNY achieved record revenue of $5.1 billion, reflecting a 9% year-over-year increase supported by robust growth across various platforms.
